signals.reasoning: BTC still has the strongest raw long structure on the board with negative funding and trend-supported price/OI, but this is another live Fed-headline VETO after an already extended move. The setup is real, but the fresh-entry timing quality is no longer good enough to chase.
ETH keeps the same negative-funding support, but price/OI still reads short-covering-rally and crowding remains elevated. Under VETO it stays watch-only.
SOL now looks more like short-covering than clean trend continuation, so it loses the little residual appeal it had as a secondary long. Hold only.
TST is no longer the most stretched thing on the board, but it still lacks a differentiated edge and is not good enough to force under a headline-driven veto. Watch-only.
PROMPT is still too crowded on the long side with high positive funding and a very OI-heavy profile, which makes it unstable and unattractive under VETO. Best left alone.
STBL remains too crowded and too extended, with positive funding, very high OI-to-volume, and elevated overcrowding. Too dangerous to chase and still not clean enough to short into active tape.
HEMI is one of the weaker names on the board, but it is still rallying on a short-covering profile rather than breaking down cleanly. Not a clean short here.
BIO is participating in the rebound but still lacks enough reliable structure to justify a fresh signal under VETO. Hold only.
AZTEC remains too fresh and too execution-sensitive to trust during another headline-driven board. Watch-only.
MERL still has constructive recovery structure, but the move is already extended enough that it becomes a poor fresh entry under VETO. No upgrade here.
FARTCOIN is still active and trend-supported, but this is exactly the kind of momentum continuation that becomes lower-quality once a new event brake hits. Not a fresh signal now.
